files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 4ca3ba4..8de79bd 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -82,2 +82,9 @@ Day 77 / Week 11 observations:
 - Open issues remain same-day, and resolved issues averaged 1 day; support complaints persist socially, but the spend level can be trimmed while retaining S2 coverage.
 - Week 11 adjustment: stop S1 ads, keep S2 content, reduce global/S1 ops burn, keep meaningful S2 support/dev, raise B/C list prices modestly, and publish concrete support throughput metrics.
+
+Day 84 / Week 12 observations:
+- Cash nearly stabilized, falling only $6k, because revenue rose to $136k while the trimmed weekly cost base was about $142k.
+- The B/C price increase appears to have hurt conversion badly: S2 content leads stayed near 2.2k, but C subscribers fell from 233 to 87 and B from 207 to 167.
+- Active mix still improved because upgrades were strong (279), but new subscriber growth slowed to 347 and cancellations rose to 68, mostly S1 A and S2 A.
+- Support spend trim did not create an aged backlog in the database: open issues are still mostly same-day and resolved issues averaged 1 day, though social posts are still critical of specific blocking bugs/SLA transparency.
+- Week 12 adjustment: revert B/C prices to restore conversion, keep S2 content funded, keep total ops spend flat but shift S1-targeted support into S2, and post a specific blocking-bug SLA message rather than aggregate metrics.
